name: Spanish B1 Vocabulary
description: Intermediate Spanish — CEFR B1 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: abrigo — overcoat. Also: warm clothes, shelter, blanket, quilt, haven, harbor, aid, protection
absoluto — absolute.
absurdo — absurd, preposterous, ludicrous, ridiculous.
abundancia — abundance.
acampar — to camp; to go camping.
acceder — to accede, to agree, to concur. Also: to access, to enter; to get (into), to achieve, to back down (change one's mind)
aclarar — to clarify, clear (make clear or clearer). Also: to explain; to clarify, to water down, to rinse (soap from hands), to clear up, to set things straight, get things clear (in one's mind)
acordeón — accordion. Also: a cheat sheet folded like an accordion
actitud — posture, pose, stance. Also: attitude, disposition, policy
acumular — to accumulate (to heap up in a mass).

acusar — to accuse, charge, indict. Also: to criminate, to arraign, to prosecute, to acknowledge (to notify sender of having received), to confess, to show; to show signs of, to snitch, tattle, rat, egg, squeal
acuático — aquatic.

adaptación — adaptation (the process of adapting something to a situation). Also: adaptation (a change made to suit a condition), adaptation (the process of change that an organism undergoes), adaptation (an instance of an organism undergoing change), adaptation (the process of adapting an artistic work), adaptation (an artistic work that has been adapted)
adelante — forward (toward the front). Also: forward (into the future)
adopción — adoption (the act of adopting). Also: adoption (the state of being adopted)
adoptar — to adopt (legally take in a child from other parents). Also: to adopt (select), to take (e.g. steps, measurements, action, position or view)
adoptivo — foster, adoptive (providing parental care to unrelated children). Also: foster (receiving such care)
adornar — to adorn, to ornament, to decorate, to garnish, to embellish, to dress, to bedeck, to deck. Also: to trim (e.g. a Christmas tree), to cheat on someone, to buy off; to bribe
adquirir — to acquire. Also: to buy
agresividad — aggressiveness.
aguacate — avocado (fruit). Also: avocado (tree), a shade of green like an avocado, a loose and lively person, policeman, cop
agujero — hole (hollow place or cavity). Also: pincushion (device that holds sewing pins), unjustified imbalance, debt
ahorrar — to save (to store for future use). Also: to save (to conserve or prevent the wasting of), to avoid, to spare
ajeno — of others, belonging to someone else. Also: distant, alien, foreign, extraneous, external
ají — pepper.
alargar — to lengthen. Also: to stretch, to extend
alcanzar — to reach; to attain or achieve; to catch up with. Also: to be enough; to suffice, to peak, to peak at

alfajor — A popular confection in Andalusia usually made with honey, almonds, nuts, and spices such as clove and coriander.. Also: alfajor (a popular confection in South America usually filled with dulce de leche)
alrededor — surroundings.
alternativa — alternative, option. Also: alternation, ceremony in which a novillero becomes a matador for the first time, when the padrino allows him to make his first kill
amargo — bitter, sour (having an acrid taste).
amenazar — to threaten.
anciano — elderly. Also: ancient
ansiedad — anxiety.
antepasado — before last.
anónimo — anonymous.
apasionante — exciting, interesting, heady.
apenas — barely, scarcely. Also: recently, just, as soon as
apetito — appetite.
apropiar — to give, bestow. Also: to seize, take hold of

apuntar — to point, to aim, to take aim (a weapon). Also: to point out, to suggest, to hint, to underline, to mark (text), to note down, to write down, to outline, to sketch, to baste (to sew with long or loose stitches), to tack, to fasten temporarily, to patch, to darn, to sew, to mend, to sharpen (a weapon or tool), to prompt, to cue, to whisper the answer, to bet, to put up money, to stake (only in certain card games), to sign up, to be in, be down, be game, to chalk up, to notch (a win, a victory, points, success)
argumento — argument. Also: plot, story line
arma — weapon, arm.

armar — to arm (supply with weapons). Also: to arm (provide with tools or skills), to assemble, to organize, get going, to provoke; stir up, to set up; install, to load (a weapon), to stock up, to balk, to pluck up
aro — hoop. Also: large ring, earring
arqueólogo — archeologist.
arrugar — to crease, to crumple. Also: to wrinkle, to chicken out, back down, lose one's nerve
asado — roast. Also: grilled, broiled

asegurar — to secure (to make fast; to close or confine effectually). Also: to assure (to give (someone) confidence in the trustworthiness of), to claim, to maintain, to ensure, to guarantee (to make a pledge to someone), to make sure, to ensure (to take care that something happens), to insure (to provide for compensation if some specified risk occurs), to belay
asesinato — assassination. Also: murder, homicide
asesino — murderer, killer (person who commits murder). Also: assassin, traitor
asimismo — likewise (expressing continuity or similarity).
asociar — to associate.
atacar — to attack, to come at. Also: to engage (enter into conflicted with), to assail

atar — to tie, tie up, tie down, to tether (secure (something) by rope or the like). Also: to constrain, limit (prevent or remove movement, leave (someone or something) without freedom of movement or ability to act)
atascar — to jam, obstruct. Also: to get stuck
atento — attentive. Also: polite, courteous, thoughtful
aumento — increase. Also: raise
auto — a public deed or ceremony. Also: order, writ, resolution, short play from the Middle Ages or the Renaissance
autoridad — authority (the power to enforce rules or give orders). Also: authority (persons in command), authority (a person accepted as a source of reliable information on a subject)
azafrán — saffron crocus (Crocus sativus). Also: saffron, turmeric (Curcuma longa)
azteca — Aztec. Also: Mexican, related to Mexico
aéreo — aerial.
balón — ball. Also: balloon
bastón — walking stick, staff, cane. Also: baton (in a marching band), riband, rod cell

bañera — bathtub, bath. Also: cockpit, the bed of a dump truck, female equivalent of bañero (“lifeguard”), A type of torture consisting of keeping someone's head underwater for long periods of time without drowning them.
beneficio — benefit. Also: profit
bicho — bug. Also: vermin, beast (non-human animal), vulva, youngster, kid (boy, or young man)
bienvenida — welcome. Also: greeting
bigote — moustache, mustache; moustaches, mustaches. Also: whisker, whiskers (used when it's large and thick) (often in plural)
boliche — bowling. Also: bowling alley, small shop, small shop, disco, club, nightclub, soapberry, low-quality tobacco, cup-and-ball
boludo — moronic, immature, foolish. Also: wealthy, lazy, having the shape of a bola (ball, sphere)
bolígrafo — ballpoint pen. Also: drunkard (drunk person)
bordado — embroidered.
bordar — to embroider (to stitch a decorative design on fabric with needle and thread of various colours). Also: to ace (to do something very well)
borracho — drunk, inebriated. Also: boozy (containing alcohol)
bota — boot.
bretón — Breton.
breve — brief, short.

brindar — to toast, drink (a toast) to (to engage in a salutation and/or accompanying raising of glasses while drinking alcohol in honor of someone or something). Also: to offer, provide, bring (to give what is needed or desired), to yield (to produce as a return), to dedicate (to designate (a bull) as being for another person as a mark of respect or affection), to offer (to propose or express one's willingness (to do something))
buque — ship, vessel.
buscador — seeker. Also: prospector
buzón — mailbox (US), letterbox (UK). Also: box, big mouth
básico — basic (elementary, simple, fundamental). Also: basic (of or pertaining to a base; having a pH greater than 7)
búsqueda — search. Also: quest
caballero — horseman. Also: knight; cavalier, gentleman, cowboy
cabello — hair (the mass of hairs on the head).

cabina — cabin (the passenger area of an airplane). Also: cabin (a private room on a ship), cockpit (the space for those in control of a vessel), cab (the space for the driver and copilots in a lorry or train), booth (an enclosure just big enough to accommodate one person)
cafetero — coffee.
cajero — cashier.
calavera — skull. Also: death's-head hawkmoth, taillight
calcular — to calculate.
camión — lorry (UK); truck (US). Also: bus

campana — bell. Also: a bell-shaped (or roughly) object or component (such as the canopy of a parachute), hood (device to suck away smokes and fumes), extractor hood, cloche, tableware cover, usually metalic
campeón — champion, champ (informal address).
candidato — candidate.
cansancio — tiredness.
capaz — able, capable.
capitán — captain (leader).
característico — characteristic, typical.

cargar — to load (to put a load on or in (a means of conveyance or a place of storage)). Also: to load (to fill (a firearm or artillery) with munition), to load (to insert (an item or items) into an apparatus so as to ready it for operation, such as a reel of film into a camera, sheets of paper into a printer etc), to load (to load a software into the primary memory), to carry (to lift (something) and take it to another place; to transport (something) by lifting), to charge (to replenish energy to (a battery, or a device containing a battery) by use of an electrical device plugged into a power outlet), to annoy, pester, to blitz, to break, ruin, to take down, kill
carrito — shopping cart. Also: diminutive of carro (“small cart”)
cartera — wallet, billfold. Also: handbag, portfolio, position (post of responsibility), female equivalent of cartero, female letter carrier
cartero — postman.
causar — to cause, to make. Also: to wreak, to bring, to provoke, to inflict
centrar — to focus, to center. Also: cross (pass the ball from one side of the pitch to the other side)
cerdo — pig, hog. Also: pork, slob, pig, hog
ceremonia — ceremony.
chiste — joke (told joke). Also: essence, importance, matter or purpose of something
chocar — to crash. Also: to bump (into), to collide (with), to shock, scandalize, to crash into, to hit, to slam into, to collide with, to annoy, to bother, to upset
chorizo — a type of sausage. Also: a foolish person, a , corruption
ciclo — cycle. Also: series, period
cilantro — coriander, cilantro.
circunstancia — circumstance.
clandestino — clandestine.
climático — climatic.
cobrar — to charge (money, etc.). Also: to collect, to gather up (strength, bravery etc.), to pay for something (get one's dues), to claim (lives)

cocer — to boil. Also: to cook (only used in situations where the food being prepared is submitted to fire; such as through the processes of boiling, simmering or steaming anything; or baking bread slowly in an oven), to brew, to chafe (get sore)
colesterol — cholesterol.
colina — hill.
combinación — combination.
cometer — to commit. Also: to make (an error, etc)
comisaría — police station.
compensar — to compensate, make up for. Also: to compensate, remunerate
competir — to compete.
comprometer — to promise, to engage to be married. Also: to compromise, to endanger
comunista — communist, Communist. Also: communistic
concebir — to conceive, imagine. Also: to understand, to conceive, become pregnant
conceder — to grant, to concede, to admit, to bestow. Also: to agree about
concentración — concentration (all senses). Also: demonstration, protest
conciencia — conscience. Also: consciousness
confirmación — confirmation.
conflicto — conflict.
congreso — congress. Also: assembly, conference, meeting
conquistador — conqueror, conquistador. Also: womanizer
consecuencia — consequence.
contaminación — pollution; contamination.
contaminante — contaminating.
contenedor — container, cargo container, shipping container. Also: dumpster (US, Canada), skip (UK, Australia)
continuación — continuation.
contribuir — to contribute.
convencer — to convince. Also: to persuade
convivir — to cohabit, to live together.
correspondiente — respective. Also: corresponding
corriente — flowing, running. Also: current (existing or occurring at the moment), common, usual, ordinary, normal
cosecha — harvest, crop. Also: vintage (of wine)
coser — to sew.
cosmético — cosmetic.
creatividad — creativity.
creencia — belief.
criar — to rear or raise (e.g., a child). Also: to grow up
cristiano — Christian (member of the Christian religion). Also: guy (person)
crítica — criticism. Also: flack, flak
crónica — chronicle.
cuaderno — notebook.
cuadra — city block. Also: stable, ward; dormitory, a unit of length and area, roughly equivalent to 100 meters or 1 hectare, respectively, although the exact size differs from country to country
cuate — twin (fraternal). Also: buddy, friend
cucharada — a spoonful, tablespoon: the quantity held in a tablespoon.
cuchillo — knife. Also: blade of knife
cuelga — hanging (especially of dried fruit).
culpa — fault. Also: guilt, blame
culpable — guilty, culpable. Also: guilty (having a sense of guilt)
cultivo — crop. Also: growing, cultivation, culture (of bacteria)
curiosidad — curiosity.
cálido — warm.
cárcel — prison.
célebre — celebrated. Also: famous
código — code. Also: cheat sheet
cómic — comic (cartoon story).
dama — lady, dame. Also: a female member of the court of honor at a quinceañera, queen, king
daño — damage. Also: hex, curse
declaración — statement. Also: declaration, tax return
decorativo — decorative.
defensa — defense. Also: retaining wall, bumper
definitivo — definitive. Also: conclusive
deformación — deformation.
degustación — the action of tasting, sampling.
delantero — front. Also: forward
delito — crime; offense.
democracia — democracy (rule by the people). Also: democracy (government under the rule of its people)
denominar — to name; to call; to designate.
denunciar — to denounce. Also: to accuse, to report, to inform against/on (e.g., a crime), to reveal, give away
depositar — to deposit, to put, to place. Also: to place, to put (e.g. faith, trust, confidence, hope, energy, time)
depresión — depression. Also: depression (area of low air pressure)

desagradable — unpleasant, disagreeable, distasteful, off-putting, displeasing, unpleasing, bad, rude, unsavory, uncomfortable, unlikable, objectionable, painful, uncongenial, crappy, gnarly (US slang). Also: nasty, ugly, unkind, objectionable, unsightly (e.g., person, behavior)
descendiente — descendant. Also: offspring
desconfiar — to distrust, to mistrust.
desfilar — to parade.
desfile — parade.
desgracia — disgrace. Also: misfortune, bad luck, accident, disaster
desmayar — to cause a person to faint. Also: to dishearten, to faint (lose consciousness)
desnudo — naked, nude, undressed. Also: bare, dispossessed, destitute, plain, clear, manifest
desorden — disorder. Also: untidiness
despertador — alarm clock.
detergente — detergent.
diablo — devil. Also: crowbar, wrecking bar, jemmy, jimmy, prybar, pinchbar, nail bar
difundir — to spread, to disseminate (ideas, etc.).
difunto — deceased.
disfraz — disguise. Also: costume
disfrazar — to disguise (to change the appearance). Also: to dress up as
disminuir — to decrease.
dispositivo — appliance, device, equipment. Also: presence (of police forces)
distinguir — to distinguish, discern. Also: to differentiate, set apart, to make out, manage to see, hear etc., to stand out, be distinguishable or differentiable, to distinguish oneself
diversidad — diversity.
divorcio — divorce (the legal dissolution of a marriage). Also: divorce (a separation of connected things)
doméstico — domestic, household, home.
débil — weak.
década — decade.
ecosistema — ecosystem.
eficiente — efficient.
elefante — elephant.
elevar — to elevate. Also: to raise; lift up, to raise (a number) (to a power)
eliminar — to eliminate.
elogiar — to praise, commend, compliment, eulogise.
embarazar — to impregnate, make pregnant. Also: to become pregnant, to incommode, annoy
embarcar — to embark, to sail, to ship out. Also: to board, to load (persons or cargo), to ship, to embark, go on, to let down someone
emigrante — emigrant.
emisora — radio station.
enamorado — infatuated, having a crush on, feeling love or attraction for another. Also: enamored, in love, smitten
encantado — enchanted, charmed. Also: pleased, delighted, thrilled, overjoyed, bewitched, haunted

encerrar — to lock up, to coop up. Also: to lock away, put away, to imprison, to nail (in a prison or mental hospital), to enclose, to confine, to immure, to contain, to hold, to include, to encapsulate, to entail, to stalemate, to separate (between brackets, commas), to take over; occupy (a building), to shut oneself up, to withdraw, to shut away oneself, to shut off oneself, to lock up or lock down oneself, to close off oneself, to shy away
encima — on top. Also: on oneself, over oneself, additionally, nevertheless, still, to top it off, on top of that
enemigo — adverse; hostile.

enfrentar — to confront, to face, to stand up to, (a person or force aggressively). Also: to face, to confront, to address, to deal with, to meet, to tackle, to cope with, to handle, to contend, to contend with (e.g. reality, an issue, a problem), to counter, to combat (e.g. a threat, terrorism, criminal activity), to pit (two sides against each other), to take on, to face off, to face off against, to fight, to engage, to clash (e.g. take on somebody in a challenge) (+ a), to face, to be facing, to be dealing with, to be up against (+ a)
engañar — to trick, to deceive, to hoodwink, to cheat. Also: to cheat on, to be unfaithful to one's spouse, to blindside, to fool oneself, to kid oneself
entonación — intonation (the rise and fall of the voice in speaking).
entrenar — to train.
entrevistar — to interview.
entusiasmar — to excite. Also: to get excited
entusiasmo — enthusiasm.
envolver — to wrap; to shroud. Also: to involve
equivocar — to mistake, mix up. Also: to make a mistake
erupción — eruption. Also: rash

escala — ladder (a frame, usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, used for ascent and descent). Also: scale (an ordered, usually numerical sequence used for measurement, means of assigning a magnitude), scale (a series of notes spanning an octave), ladder, hierarchy (of a company, of the military), scale (the ratio of depicted distance to actual distance), scale (size; scope)
escena — scene. Also: stage, cutscene

escrito — a writing, a writ. Also: a typescript, embossed or pressed manuscript or document, a literary or scientific journal entry, a legal demand or allegation with regards to a cause or disagreement
escritura — document, deed. Also: writing, scripture
esfuerzo — effort, endeavor.
eslogan — slogan.
espacial — spatial (pertaining to the dimension of space). Also: spatial (pertaining to outer space)
especia — spice.
espléndido — splendid, magnificent, splendiferous, superb, lavish, glorious. Also: gorgeous
esqueleto — skeleton. Also: frame (structural elements of a constructed object)
estacionar — to park (to bring a vehicle to a halt in a specified place).
estereotipo — stereotype.
estimular — to stimulate.
estresado — stressed.
estropear — to ruin, spoil. Also: to break down (to stop working), to go bad, spoil (of food)
estructura — structure.
estético — aesthetic.
estúpido — stupid.
etiqueta — label, tag. Also: price tag, tag, etiquette
evolución — evolution. Also: development
excusa — excuse, cop-out. Also: pretext
existencia — existence. Also: stock; inventory
explorar — to explore.
exponer — to exhibit. Also: to expose
extinción — extinction (the action of making or becoming extinct; annihilation).
extraer — to extract. Also: to take out
facha — appearance, looks, style. Also: a person's face, clothing that's either in poor condition or not appropriate for some occasion
facilitar — to facilitate (to make easier). Also: to provide, to send, to deliver
falda — skirt. Also: woman, bit of skirt (woman as an object of desire), brisket (a cut of meat), mountainside
favor — favor/favour.
favorecer — to support. Also: to favor / favour (show beneficence toward), to suit (to be suitable or apt for one's image)
ficción — fiction.

ficha — chip, token (e.g. used in gambling, games and for payphones). Also: index card, leaflet, sheet, factsheet (sheet of paper containing information), headshot, voucher, piece (in games like checkers, dominos, backgammon, etc.), counter (in games)
fijo — fixed. Also: firm, concrete, tangible
filósofo — philosopher.
flaco — a thin man. Also: used to address someone without using their name, a young man, boyfriend
flauta — flute. Also: a type of fried cylindrical tortilla or taco, baguette (long thin loaf of bread)
fomentar — to foster, encourage, promote, advance. Also: to feed, nourish, to provide, to apply a poultice, to foment (excite)
fonético — phonetic.
formalidad — formality.
fragmento — fragment. Also: clip (a frame containing a number of bullets which is intended to be inserted into the magazine of a firearm to allow for rapid reloading.)
funcionamiento — functioning.
fusión — fusion. Also: melting, merger, merge
fértil — fertile.
gallego — Galician. Also: Spaniard
gastronómico — gastronomic, gastronomical.
generalizar — to generalize.
generar — to generate, to create, to produce, to cause. Also: to engender, to breed
genético — genetic.
gigantesco — gigantic, gigantesque; of a giant.
gimnasia — gymnastics.
gira — tour (a journey through a list of places).
gol — goal.
gordo — fat man. Also: the grand prize in a lottery, honey, feces, the act of defecation, number two
gota — a drop or small amount of. Also: eyedrops, gout, goutte
gozar — to enjoy oneself. Also: to enjoy

grano — grain (the seed of various grass food crops). Also: grain, seed, kernel, bean (a single seed of certain crops), grain (a single similar particle of various substances), pimple, blackhead (a blocked skin pore, typically inflamed, painful, and filled with pus), point (the main intent or focus of a conversation), grain (the linear texture of a material or surface, especially wood), grain (flawed visual texture present in most processed photographic film), grano, Spanish grain (a traditional small unit of mass, equivalent to about 50 mg), grain (any of various traditional units of mass notionally based on the weight of different grains)
grapa — staple (fastener). Also: clamp, stitch
grasa — fat or grease used in cooking. Also: nutritional fat
guardia — watch, guard, guarding, guard duty, sentry duty. Also: guard (unit or squad), custody, shift (in certain professions such as for medical staff), guard (ground grappling position)
guindilla — chili pepper.
guión — superseded spelling of guion, deprecated in 2010 by the Royal Spanish Academy.
güero — having pale skin and/or blond hair; whitey.
habitar — to inhabit, to live or dwell in / on.
harto — tired, fed up. Also: stuffed, sated (having the appetite satisfied), many
herencia — heredity, inheritance.
herramienta — a tool, an implement.

hierro — iron (chemical element). Also: branding iron (piece of metal bent into a distinctive shape and used to brand livestock), brand (mark or scar made by burning with a branding iron), brand (symbolic identity, represented by a name or logo), iron or steel weapon, any of various objects made of iron, importance
hija — daughter; female equivalent of hijo.

hilo — thread, yarn, string. Also: linen, edge of something sharp, crosshair, reticule, ellipsis of hilo de oro, type of stinging jellyfish in Guanacaste, Costa Rica, thread (series of messages), helix
homenaje — homage, tribute.
huerta — garden, vegetable garden. Also: orchard, cultivated land, the area of Murcia and Valencia with fertile ground
humano — human. Also: humane
identificación — identification.
ilegal — illegal, unlawful.
iluminar — to illuminate.
ilusión — illusion. Also: hope, expectation, enthusiasm, wishful thinking
impedir — to impede, hinder.
implicar — to imply, to implicate. Also: to oppose, to contradict
imponer — to impose, impress. Also: to impose, enforce, foist, to enjoin, to dominate; to show force
impresionar — to impress; to make an impression on.
impresión — impression. Also: print, shock
imprimir — to print.
incendio — fire (especially burning buildings or forests). Also: blaze, outburst
incienso — incense. Also: Myrocarpus frondosus, a tree from the Fabaceae family
incorporar — to incorporate. Also: to fold, stir gently, to stand up, to sit up
inevitable — inevitable, inescapable, unavoidable (unable to be avoided).
influir — to influence. Also: to have influence on
informe — shapeless, formless. Also: misshapen
informática — computer science, informatics, information technology. Also: female equivalent of informático, a female computer scientist
iniciar — to begin; to initiate.
iniciativa — initiative.
inmóvil — motionless, immobile, unmoving, still, stationary, static. Also: frozen (in reference to a person or creature), becalmed (e.g. a becalmed ship), immovable, unmoved (less common meaning)
innecesario — unnecessary.
inocente — innocent.
insistir — to insist.
inspiración — inspiration.
insulto — insult.

integrar — to integrate, to embed, to incorporate, to include. Also: to make up, to compose, to mainstream, to join (+ a or en) (i.e. to become part of a greater whole) (often used in business, global and political situations), to fit in (+ a or en if there is an object)
inteligencia — intelligence.
intención — intention.

interpretación — interpretation. Also: interpreting (i.e. oral translating), performance, rendition, acting, portrayal (film, theatre), acting (profession), reading, construction (i.e. interpretation of a text or action)
introducción — introduction. Also: opening (of series)
intérprete — interpreter (one who interprets speech in another language). Also: performer
invento — invention. Also: leash
irritar — to irritate. Also: to excite
izquierdo — left; on the left side or toward the left; the opposite of right. Also: left-handed, crooked
jamás — never. Also: ever
jeta — snout. Also: sleep (act), anger face, insolence, impudence, crust, rind
juguete — toy (something to play with).
juntar — to combine, unite. Also: to gather (together); to collect, to close partway, to join together, to gather together, socialize, get together, come together, consort, to pick up (something)
jurado — juror, juryman, juryperson (member of a jury [sense 1]). Also: judge (member of a jury [sense 2]; officiator of a competitive event)
justicia — justice. Also: righteousness, the law
juvenil — juvenile. Also: teenage, youthful, in an age group in a certain sport, generally corresponding to under-16, under-17, under-18 or under-19, but it depends on the sport
lavadora — washing machine, washer, wash.
legumbre — legume. Also: vegetable

lenguaje — language (the ability to communicate using words). Also: language (a body of words, and set of methods of combining them, understood by a community), language (the expression of thought (the communication of meaning) in a specified way), vocabulary
lento — slow.
ley — law (a well-established characteristic of nature). Also: law (body of rules issued by a legislative body), law (particular piece of legislation), religion; credence; worship of a god
licor — liqueur.
limitar — to limit. Also: to abut, to border
lingüístico — linguistic.
listo — clever, smart (with ser). Also: ready, set, all set, prepared, poised, up, down (with estar), alert
llorón — crybaby, whiner.
lobo — wolf. Also: drunkenness
lograr — to get, to obtain. Also: to achieve, to manage to, to pull off, to be successful
luminoso — luminous, bright.
lágrima — tear, teardrop.
lápiz — pencil.
maduro — ripe. Also: mature, of relatively old age (said of people)
mancha — spot; stain. Also: blemish
mando — command. Also: remote control, controller, gamepad, joypad
manifestar — to manifest, reveal, display, show. Also: to declare, express, to protest
manía — mania, bad habit, obsession. Also: mania (violent derangement), mania (craze, something popular at the time), dislike, foible, quirk (unusual habit or way)
maquillar — to apply makeup or cosmetics to. Also: to fiddle, massage (numbers, data), to disguise, dress up, to put on makeup, to apply cosmetics

medio — half (one of two equal parts of any whole). Also: half (any fraction with a denominator of 2), medio, half-celemin (a traditional unit of dry measure equivalent to about 2.3 L), medio (the usual container used to measure medios), medio, half-real (a former coin), medio, half-peso (a former coin), medio, half-sucre (a former coin), medio, half-balboa (a former coin), medio, half-boliviano (a former coin), medio, half-octavo (a former coin equal to ¹⁄₁₆ real), medio (following decimalization, the notional amount of 6+¹⁄₄ centavos as half of the notional amount of a ¹⁄₈-peseta real and later by extension any 5 centavo coin), middle (the part of anything located halfway between its ends or extremes in time or along one dimension in space), midfielder, halfback (a person forming part of the middle or central defensive or offensive line, especially in soccer), synonym of médium (“medium”) (a person claiming to the ability to communicate with the dead), cut (the payment demanded to permit or facilitate some action, especially as a bribe), middle term (the general category that appears in both premises and disappears in the conclusion), mean (the second and third terms of a proportion), center, heart (the innermost part of anything with regard to all dimensions), method, way, means (the actions or things by which some goal is achieved or intended to be achieved, something serving some purpose), synonym of diligencia (“diligence, hard work”) (as the generally effective means to achieve any goal), medium (a means of communication, especially mass communication), medium (the physical space and substance through which some phenomenon occurs), medium (the physical substances with which art is made), environment (the circumstances that affect a person or animal's development), society (the circumstances in which a person or group of people lives), circle (a particular segment of society), habitat (the circumstances in which an animal or group of animals lives)

Also: bouncer, chucker-out (a member of security personnel employed by bars, nightclubs, etc to maintain order and deal with patrons who cause trouble), goalkeeper

reproducción — reproduction, procreation (the act of reproducing new individuals biologically). Also: reproduction (the act of making copies), reproduction, copy, duplicate (a copy of something, as in a piece of art; a duplicate), playback (the replaying of something previously recorded, especially sound or moving images), play (an instance of watching or listening to digital media), view (an individual viewing of a video by a user)
